author: han0110
content: Before we have the formal spec for the proof node API, the latest zkboost API doucment can be found in [`api.json`](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/han0110/5e9790e9fa224dc3aaa77264e7cbe5f3/raw/3334cdc71ba491f40732c5a5e7e47129aa2b67e8/api-v0.3.0.json) ([rendered](https://petstore.swagger.io/?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/han0110/5e9790e9fa224dc3aaa77264e7cbe5f3/raw/3334cdc71ba491f40732c5a5e7e47129aa2b67e8/api-v0.3.0.json)).

author: kevaundray
content: do you think this should go into execution-apis?

author: taulepton_
content: Execution-apis may be a suitable home for it, but I'm not certain. The main resistance I'd have to putting it in execution-apis is that execution-apis is primarily associated with EL api's, whereas this is a distinct and standalone proof node/service. As such, it may be cleaner to introduce a new repository `proof-apis` which serves as the api home for the proof node. I don't think we need to do this urgently as the API spec <@466102668138512394> provided above will allow people to build from and we don't have multiple implmentations at this point. However at some point, if we do have diversity at the proof node layer, then we should consider centralising specs.

author: kevaundray
content:
> primarily associated with EL api's,
Yeah this was my initial concern too

author: kevaundray
content: I do think creating a new repo like you mentioned `proof-apis` would be better than having a gist file right now, so there is a central place where we can find and update it
